cells.cell_text: 3. Discrepancy (% and # of subjects) between the databases a

page.content: The difference in proportions is reported as -0.010 with a confidence interval from -0.010 to -0.001, while the two-tailed p value (McNemar) is 0.031. In the second table, the data covers various parameters with the first WBC count showing 1257 subjects from the manually abstracted database and 1437 from EHR extraction, presenting a discrepancy of 3.2% (40 subjects), with manually abstracted database errors at 2.5% (32) and EHR-extracted data errors at 0.6% (8), a median discrepancy of 0.75 k/mm3 and a discrepancy range of 0.01–109 k/mm3. The 1st Hemoglobin counts 1333 and 1460 subjects respectively, with discrepancies at 11.9% (158), errors of 5.8% (77) and 8.3% (110) respectively, a median of 1.4 g/dl, range 0.1–25.9 g/dl. Peak total bilirubin has 1565 and 1336 subjects, with 11.4% (152) discrepancy, errors of 6.9% (92) and 5.1% (68), median 1.45 mg/dl, range 0.1–15.2 mg/dl. Peak direct bilirubin has 681 and 674 subjects with 4.9% (33) discrepancy and 4.5% (30) errors and PDA shows 512 and 414 subjects with 12.8% (53) discrepancies and errors at 12.8% (53).
